mlops_intro: Every model here moves through a versioned MLOps loop: training cycles are logged and evaluated against frozen baselines, promotion to champion is a human act, the triage use-case rolls out shadow-first, and live scoreboards below track both use-cases against their training-time evaluation.

live_grader.note: insufficient data (n=0) — the rolling scoreboard fills as grader predictions in data/results/predictions.jsonl gain labels in benchmark.csv

shadow_triage.banner: shadow — not steering the queue; rollout is a human decision

scoreboard.caveat: shadow — n=3 labeled shadow nights (<100); treat the uplift as provisional

champion.name: Incumbent rules
champion.note: Deterministic rule_score(); consults the excluded dur_z column by design (asymmetric — the bar the rules already clear, per EVAL-PROTOCOL-V2 §3).

verdict.text: Rules remain authoritative. The challenger does not beat the incumbent rules on grouped AUC. Promotion to champion is a human act only, and none has been made.

cycles.note: not comparable: corrupted table + protocol change
not comparable: corrupted table + protocol change
not comparable: corrupted table + protocol change

baselines.name: Majority class
baselines.note: Constant predictor — the discrimination floor (0.5 by definition).

baselines.name: scatter_pct threshold
baselines.note: The single most interpretable quality cut; logged by name every cycle.

baselines.name: Best single feature (scatter_pct)
baselines.note: Highest-AUC raw feature — the ceiling any single-cut rule could reach.

baselines.name: Incumbent rules
baselines.note: Champion. Asymmetric: consults excluded dur_z. Model must beat this on grouped AUC without excluded columns to earn promotion.

baselines.name: Challenger model (gbm)
baselines.note: Grouped-OOF headline AUC — the honest new-target generalization estimate.

model_card_html_or_md:
# Model Card — Run-Quality Model

Structured after Mitchell et al., "Model Cards for Model Reporting" (2019). This card
describes the **deployed** classifier (cycle 3) and the v2 evaluation that governs it
from the next cycle. Numbers here are reproduced from `data/results/model-metrics.csv`,
`docs/MODEL.md`, and the frozen `docs/EVAL-PROTOCOL-V2.md`.

## Model details

- **Purpose.** Predict whether one photometric reduction run is scientifically usable
  for submission, as a calibrated probability `p_good`, to help a human triage the
  review queue faster.
- **Type.** Calibrated logistic regression (scikit-learn `LogisticRegression`,
  `class_weight="balanced"`, sigmoid calibration) on a median-imputed, standardized
  feature pipeline. The ladder escalates to `HistGradientBoosting` only above 100 rows
  and only if it beats logistic in CV; today's deployed model is logistic.
- **Version / provenance.** Deployed: cycle 3 (2026-07-15), logistic, n_train=70,
  11 features. Every cycle records `schema_version`, `dataset_sha256`, and an entry in
  the hash-chained audit log (`actor="model"`).
- **Owner.** Single-maintainer citizen-science pipeline (astro project). Model artifacts
  live in `data/results/` (gitignored, local-only; pickle is produced and consumed by
  one script on one machine).
- **Features (v2, 13).** `n_frames`, `exposure_s`, `scatter_pct`, `depth_snr`,
  `rprs_rel_err`, `dur_rel_err`, `tmid_err_min`, `aperture_px`, `n_comps`,
  `resid_shapiro_p`, `resid_ljungbox_p`, `coverage_frac`, `beta_rednoise`. All are
  reduction-quality signals; none is derived from the published truth (see leakage).

## Intended use

- **In scope.** Triage and pre-ranking of the human review queue: order dossiers,
  sharpen the RECOMMEND line, and flag likely-bad reductions for a closer look. The
  output is advisory only.
- **Primary users.** The pipeline maintainer reviewing dossiers before any submission
  to AAVSO / Exoplanet Watch / MPC.
- **Out of scope — do not use for:**
  - **Auto-approval or unsupervised submission.** Nothing is submitted without a human
    setting `status: approved`. No auto-approval path exists and none may be added.
  - **Scientific claims.** `p_good` is a workflow-triage probability, not evidence about
    a planet, an ephemeris, or a detection. It must never appear in a science result.
  - **Cross-instrument or cross-target trust beyond the training distribution** (see
    biases). It has not been validated on Seestar data; on Seestar it runs in shadow
    mode only.
  - **Ranking anything other than reduction usability** (e.g. target selection — that is
    `night_score.py`).

## Factors

- **Instrument.** Training data is MicroObservatory (single 6-inch optic) plus EXOTIC
  practice/validation sets. Behaviour on other instruments is unknown.
- **Target and sky.** Northern-hemisphere targets only (HAT-P-32b, TrES-2b/3b, CoRoT-2b,
  HD 189733b, Qatar-1b/10b, WASP-2b/135b, XO-1b, HAT-P-27b/55b, KELT-20b).
- **Archive era.** Nights span 2017→present; archive-era systematics and seasonal quality
  differ. Recent-30-day MO nights skew low quality.
- **Evaluation slicing.** Grouped by `target` (extend to `(target, instrument)` once more
  than one instrument is present) — the split that matters, since deployment is always a
  new night and often a new target.

## Metrics

Discrimination is reported under **grouped** cross-validation (unseen target at test
time) as the headline, alongside the random-CV number; the gap is the target-leakage
estimate. Calibration uses a small-n (3-bin) reliability curve. Uncertainty is a
stratified bootstrap 95% CI (1000 resamples). Full definitions: `docs/EVAL-PROTOCOL-V2.md`.

**Deployed model — cycle 3 (v1 metrics, as logged):** n=70, 15 positives (21%),
random-CV AUC 0.891 ± 0.041, Brier 0.123, 10-bin ECE 0.130, reject precision 0.925.
These predate the v2 protocol (no grouped AUC, no CIs, 10-bin ECE) and are not
comparable to v2 cycles.

**v2 preview (dry-run on the current n=100 / 29-positive table, not yet a logged
cycle):** grouped-CV AUC 0.878, random-CV AUC 0.886, gap 0.008 (low target leakage);
grouped AUC 95% CI [0.808, 0.942]; Brier 95% CI [0.086, 0.193]; 3-bin ECE 0.085.
Baselines on the same data: majority 0.50, `scatter_pct` 0.878, best single feature
`depth_snr` 0.899, incumbent rules 0.924. **The model does not yet beat `depth_snr`
alone or the rules on grouped AUC** — this is exactly why the rules stay authoritative
and the human gate is non-negotiable. The first *logged* v2 cycle is triggered by the
orchestrator at ≥ 25 positives with all baselines present.

## Evaluation & training data

- **Source.** MicroObservatory public archive nights reduced by the pipeline and scored
  against published ExoClock ephemerides (`mo_benchmark.py`); EXOTIC practice/validation
  runs with known truth; and human review decisions. One row per documented run
  (`data/records/<id>/record.json` → `data/results/run-features.csv`).
- **Size & balance.** v2 rebuild: 100 trainable rows, 29 positive / 71 negative
  (~29% positive). Deployed model trained on 70 / 15 positive.
- **Labels.** Positive = `ACCURATE` / `MARGINAL` / human-`approved`; negative =
  `INACCURATE` / `NON-DETECTION` / `RUN-FAILED` / human-`rejected` / `NOISY-CONSISTENT`.
  Labels inherit the scoring thresholds in `mo_benchmark.py`: the model learns *our*
  definition of "usable," not a platonic one. The definition is versioned in that file.
- **Known biases.**
  - **Single-instrument optics** — MicroObservatory 6-inch dominates; the model may key
    on MO characteristics. Breaking this correlation is the whole point of phase P3
    (cross-instrument) before P4 trusts it on Seestar.
  - **Northern targets only** — no Southern-sky validation.
  - **Archive-era effects** — systematics and quality vary by era/season; good labels
    come disproportionately from the deep archive and practice sets, bad labels from the
    poor recent season.
  - **Small positive count** — grouped AUC and calibration are fragile until positives
    grow well past 25.

## Quantitative analyses

- Random vs grouped AUC and their gap are logged every cycle (`cv_auc_random`,
  `cv_auc_grouped`, `cv_auc_gap`) — the leakage diagnostic.
- Reliability curve per cycle in `data/results/calibration/<cycle_utc>.json`.
- Bootstrap CIs on grouped AUC and Brier per cycle.
- Per-feature imputed fraction per cycle (a feature going all-NaN is a broken emitter).

## Ethical & operational considerations

- **The human gate is load-bearing.** The pipeline's core invariant is *automate
  reduction, never judgment.* The model compresses review time; it does not replace the
  reviewer. `.claude/settings.json` denies submission side-effects to automation, and no
  auto-approval exists.
- **Failure mode that matters.** A false SCREEN-PASS that lulls the reviewer is worse
  than a false REJECT (a wasted look). Thresholds are asymmetric (reject liberally at
  p<0.35, pass rarely at p>0.90) and `screen_pass_rate` is expected near zero until
  calibration matures.
- **No consensus contamination.** The model is not used to generate Zooniverse
  classifications or any crowd-consensus input.
- **Probabilities carry context.** `p_good` is always quoted with `n_train` in dossiers
  so a small-data probability is not over-read.

## Caveats & maintenance

- **Retrain cadence.** Weekly, after the Tuesday benchmark top-up and nightly backfill
  land new labels; a cycle is only logged at ≥ 25 positives with all baselines present.
- **Drift monitoring.** Per-cycle `dataset_sha256` (training-set change detection),
  imputed-fraction (emitter health), grouped-AUC stability across cycles, and the
  calibration JSON. Schema assertions refuse to train on a drifted feature table.
- **Trust gate for P4 (Seestar shadow → weighted RECOMMEND, still never approval):**
  ECE < 0.10, ≥ 25 positives, stable grouped AUC across ≥ 3 consecutive cycles.
- **Cycle comparability.** Cycles 1–3 were trained on a feature table that was later
  found corrupted (benchmark.csv drift, fixed 2026-07-15) and used v1 metrics; they are
  not comparable to v2 cycles. See `docs/MODEL.md`.
limitations: Single-instrument optics: training data is dominated by MicroObservatory's 6-inch optic (plus EXOTIC practice sets). Behaviour on other instruments — including Seestar — is unvalidated; on Seestar the model runs in shadow mode only.
Northern-hemisphere targets only; no Southern-sky validation.
Small positive count (~30). Grouped AUC and calibration are fragile until positives grow well past 25; the grouped-AUC CI is correspondingly wide.

The challenger does not yet beat the incumbent rules — nor even the best single feature (depth_snr) — on grouped AUC. That is exactly why the rules stay authoritative and the human review gate is non-negotiable.
Archive-era systematics: nights span 2017-> and quality varies by era/season; good labels come disproportionately from the deep archive, bad labels from the poor recent season.
Calibration ECE is a small-n 3-bin curve — deliberately coarse, and NOT comparable to the 10-bin ECE of cycles 1-3.

The rules baseline is intentionally asymmetric: rule_score() legitimately consults the excluded column dur_z, so baseline_rules_auc is 'the bar the rules already clear,' not an apples-to-apples model comparison (see EVAL-PROTOCOL-V2 §3).

p_good is a workflow-triage probability, never evidence about a planet, an ephemeris, or a detection. It must never appear in a science result, and nothing is submitted without a human setting status: approved.
reproduce_cmds: .venv/bin/python scripts/ml_features.py train     # log a cycle (>=25 pos, all baselines)
mlflow ui --backend-store-uri data/mlruns         # browse logged runs (if MLflow installed)
